title: Dead Air in the Flat
openingIncident: At 8:55 pm, Eleanor Whitaker, renowned documentary filmmaker, was found dead in the modest living room, its sofa flanked by a coffee table littered with a shoe, a remote control, a water bottle and a backpack. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Haines
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, Trevor Langford helped me move the shoe aside. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Miriam Clarke and Eleanor Whitaker were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Trevor Langford and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

suspects.name: Miriam Clarke
suspects.statement: I left at 8:40 pm. I never touched the water bottle and did not go near the remote control.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:40 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Trevor Langford
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, while Gareth Haines moved the shoe aside, I moved the remote control aside. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:55 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Gareth Haines and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

clues.title: Granules in Water Bottle
clues.detail: Undissolved blue granules cling below the liquid line inside the water bottle. The same blue residue is visible at Eleanor Whitaker's lips and in the remaining drink.

clues.title: Position of Shoe
clues.detail: The fresh contact mark left by the shoe is crossed by a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink whose edge remains unbroken. Gareth Haines and Trevor Langford both state that they moved the shoe aside at 8:45 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Water Bottle
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught around the handle and rim of the water bottle. Miriam Clarke's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Position of Remote Control
clues.detail: The fresh contact mark left by the remote control is crossed by a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink. Trevor Langford states that they moved the remote control aside at 8:45 pm immediately before leaving the room.

clues.title: Mark on Backpack
clues.detail: A dark mark crosses the backpack. The same intact factory finish continues over the marked area.

solution.motive: Miriam Clarke killed Eleanor Whitaker because the discovery that Eleanor had secretly siphoned the production grant into her personal account.
solution.method: A fatal crushed dose was dissolved in the water bottle before Eleanor Whitaker drank.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:45 pm and before discovery at 8:55 pm
solution.opportunity: Miriam Clarke claimed to have left at 8:40 pm. The physical trace on the shoe corroborates the action described by Gareth Haines, while the trace on the remote control corroborates the action described by Trevor Langford. They left together at 8:45 pm, and both traces are crossed by evidence from the later attack. Meanwhile, fine copper-coloured silk threads on the water bottle directly contradict Miriam Clarke's denial and place them at the murder object during the attack window.
